name: Firstfall
description: Port Meridian — called Firstfall by everyone who actually lives here. One year old, ~100 survivors, and the planet pressing in from every direction.

object_templates.room_look: A handwritten note rests on the last chair.
object_templates.description: Neat handwriting on a torn corner of something larger. 'The slides are wrong. Find Elder Rourke in the settler section of town. Listen to everything.' No signature. The ink is smeared at one corner — water, or something else.

object_templates.room_look: A photograph lies face-down on an empty bunk.
object_templates.description: A printed photograph, kept face-down. When you turn it over: three people and a coastline that has no chance of being this one. The colors are oversaturated the way Earth cameras always were. Nobody is looking at the camera. On the back, in a hand different from the note in the briefing room: 'Thessaly Bay, 2173. We'll go back.'

object_templates.room_look: A dog-eared paperback novel sits on the card table.
object_templates.description: A physical book — paper, ink, binding glue. Someone brought this from Earth. It weighs almost nothing and costs a staggering amount in cargo allowance to justify. The spine is cracked at three places. The last fifty pages are unread. The bookmark is a boarding pass from a transit hub that no longer exists.

object_templates.room_look: A sealed bottle of Earth whiskey sits on the rail.
object_templates.description: Unopened. The seal is still intact. Aged twelve years on a world four light-years from here. The label is water-stained but the contents are not. Someone brought this and has not opened it. The fact that it is sitting here unguarded says something about whoever left it, or about what they decided.

areas.name: South Gate — Interior
areas.description: The inner face of the settlement's south entrance. The wall is Corporate security fencing bolted to salvaged structural supports — higher than it needs to be and not quite high enough. A guard post stands to one side, occupied by someone who has seen enough new arrivals to know which ones will last. A processing facility opens ahead to the north. The gate mechanism stands to the south.

areas.name: The Receiving — Intake Lobby
areas.description: A prefab waiting room with plastic chairs and a counter nobody looks happy to be on either side of. A hand-drawn sign reads: WELCOME TO PORT MERIDIAN. Someone has crossed out PORT MERIDIAN and written FIRSTFALL in large letters. A second sign below it: ALL NEW ARRIVALS MUST COMPLETE INTAKE PROCESSING BEFORE ACCESSING COLONY FACILITIES. Processing continues north through a door. The gate is back to the south.

areas.name: The Receiving — Assessment Office
areas.description: A narrow office where a Corporate clerk verifies your transit manifest and issues a colony ID chip. The walls are papered with regulations nobody reads and a map of the settlement that's already out of date. The clerk processes arrivals with the efficiency of someone who stopped caring about individuals sometime in the last six months.

areas.name: The Receiving — Briefing Room
areas.description: Rows of folding chairs face a screen displaying a Corporate orientation presentation on loop. The slides haven't been updated since before the colony launched — they describe a thriving settlement of several hundred, optimistic projections, resource targets. Someone has left a handwritten note on the last chair: 'The slides are wrong. Find Elder Rourke in the settler section of town. Listen to everything.'

state_lines.default: The orientation screen cycles through slides of a colony that no longer exists.
state_lines.active: The orientation screen runs a Corporate welcome loop — optimistic, pre-landing, incorrect.

fixtures.look: A wall-mounted screen showing the Corporate colony orientation presentation. The date stamp in the corner is from before the Perihelion made landfall.
fixtures.examine: Slide 14 of 22. 'Port Meridian is projected to house 800 colonists by Year Two, supported by a robust supply chain and established resource extraction operations.' The projection date is six months ago. The settlement currently holds fewer than 100 people. Someone has written 'WRONG' in marker across the bottom of the screen.

areas.name: The Receiving — Medical Screening
areas.description: A basic examination room where a medic checks your vitals after cryo-revival. The equipment is quality Corporate issue. The medic is thorough and quiet, noting things in a data pad without commenting. On the wall, a faded poster about symbiont exposure protocols has been defaced with one word over the original text: INEVITABLE.

areas.name: The Receiving — Equipment Issue
areas.description: A supply window where new arrivals receive the standard-issue colony kit — a basic survival pack, emergency rations, a comm unit, and a printed copy of the colonial regulation summary. The supply officer moves fast and expects you to keep up. The kit is adequate. It is not enough.

areas.name: The Receiving — Assignment Board
areas.description: The final room of intake processing. A large board covers one wall — bunk assignments, work postings, safety notices, a roster of the missing with no dates next to the names. A door to the north opens onto the settlement proper, the main street stretching away into the small, raw colony that is the only human place on this planet. The processing corridor is back to the south. Welcome to Firstfall.

areas.name: Main Street — South End
areas.description: The southern stretch of the settlement's main thoroughfare — a packed-dirt track between prefab buildings. This end of town is quietest, mostly storage and overflow from intake processing. Corporate gray on every building. The street runs north. Official warehouse signage marks a building entrance to the east. Warmth and the smell of real coffee drift from the west.

areas.name: Main Street — Central
areas.description: The heart of the main street. More people pass through here than anywhere else in the colony — Corporate staff, settlers, the occasional Adapted who moves through the crowd without quite touching anyone. Notice boards on both sides carry work offers, warnings, missing persons, and at least one unsigned accusation that Corporate has been falsifying the casualty reports. The most finished building in the settlement — clean prefab panels, a corporate seal visible at the entrance — stands to the east. The sound of fabrication machinery and the smell of hot metal carry from the west.

areas.name: Main Street — North End
areas.description: The northern stretch of main street, where the Corporate prefabs give way to settler construction — rough-cut local timber framing, salvaged panel walls, roofs patched with whatever was available. A clean, well-maintained structure with a medical cross above the door stands to the east. Bunk windows and the low murmur of a common room mark the housing block to the west. The perimeter wall and its working gate are directly ahead to the north.

areas.name: North Gate
areas.description: A heavy working gate — manual mechanism, built to stay closed if power fails. The frame carries a list of resource runner team assignments, most crossed off. The planet presses in from beyond: darker soil, jutting mineral formations, alien scrubland that has never accommodated humans without objection. Not all the runners who went through this gate came back. The settlement extends behind you to the south. The wall top runs west.

areas.name: Supply Depot — Sales Floor
areas.description: Corporate supply, the official store. The shelves are organized with the precision of someone who knows exactly how little they have and needs everyone else to know it too. Pricing is posted in corporate credits — high but consistent. The selection depends entirely on what came off the last shuttle. There is always less than you need. A storage bay is accessible to the east. The street entrance is west.

areas.name: Supply Depot — Storage
areas.description: The back warehouse. Locked shelving units line the walls, most of them less full than the manifest claims. Crates stenciled with corporate shipping codes are stacked with the kind of care that suggests someone knows the contents are irreplaceable. The entrance is west.

areas.name: Corporate Administration — Lobby
areas.description: The administrative hub of the colony — the most finished building in Firstfall, which still means prefab panels and fluorescent lighting, but clean and maintained. A reception desk controls access. The corporate seal on the wall looks out of place here, like a formal portrait in a survival shelter. Corridors branch north, east, and south to various offices. The street entrance is west.

areas.name: Corporate Administration — Commerce Office
areas.description: Trade licensing, contract registration, corporate employment postings, and the permit desk where independent traders register their operations. The walls are covered in regulatory notices and fee schedules. The clerk will tell you that everything is available with the right paperwork and the right credits, in that order.

areas.name: Corporate Administration — Security Office
areas.description: Firstfall's law enforcement, such as it is — two corporate security staff and a wall of camera feeds showing the gate checkpoints and main street. The missing persons board here is more complete than the one on main street and significantly more grim. Communications equipment is visible through the north door.

areas.name: Corporate Administration — Records Room
areas.description: Filing cabinets, data terminals, and the colony's official history — which diverges from the one settlers tell in ways that are not subtle. Transit manifests, casualty logs, resource reports. Some terminals require clearance. Some cabinets are locked. The original landing roster is here: six hundred and forty names, most with a date recorded in the status column.

state_lines.default: The original landing roster sits open on the table — six hundred and forty names, most with a date in the status column.

fixtures.look: A printed binder, thick with pages. The cover reads: PERIHELION COLONIAL MANIFEST — AUTHORIZED PERSONNEL ONLY. The binder is not locked. The warning is decoration.
fixtures.examine: Six hundred and forty names, in alphabetical order by family name. Each entry: name, colony assignment, current status. Most status entries contain a date. The dates are not arrival dates — the column header reads 'CLOSED.' The word appears 547 times. Eighty-nine entries have no date in that column. Eighty-nine people are still here.
fixtures.read: The dates cluster in the first three months. Weeks four through eight are the worst. Then a long plateau of smaller numbers. Then the pace slows — not because things got safer, but because there were fewer people left to lose.

areas.name: Corporate Administration — Communications Center
areas.description: The quantum comms relay hub — the only connection to the rest of human civilization. The equipment is significant and maintained with obsessive care. Messages to Earth take weeks. Messages from Earth arrive on the same schedule, carrying news, orders, and quarterly reports from people who have no idea what is happening here. Access is logged. Comms Officer Valdez is almost always at her station.

areas.name: The Canteen — Main Hall
areas.description: The colony's mess hall and its only true common space — the one place where Corporate staff, settlers, Adapted, and new arrivals occupy the same room without it being a confrontation. The food is adequate. The coffee is real Earth-grown and therefore precious. The conversations are where you learn what is actually happening. Notice boards, mismatched chairs, a counter where Cook Fatima runs things with total authority. Cool alien air moves in through an open doorway to the west. A different architectural quality — rougher, warmer, more human — begins through the south passage.

state_lines.default: The coffee urn on the counter gurgles quietly — real Earth-grown, the smell impossible to miss.
state_lines.empty: The coffee urn on the counter sits empty, the bottom faintly scorched.
state_lines.full: The coffee urn on the counter gurgles quietly — real Earth-grown, the smell impossible to miss.

fixtures.look: A large insulated urn, corporate issue but well-maintained. A hand-lettered sign above it reads: ONE CUP PER PERSON PER SHIFT. The sign has been partially obscured by another sign that reads: FATIMA'S RULES, NOT CORPORATE'S.
fixtures.examine: Earth-grown coffee beans, ground here. The supply is rationed because there is no resupply until the next shuttle. Nobody knows when the next shuttle is. The urn is treated with a degree of care usually reserved for medical equipment.

state_lines.default: The notice board is dense with postings — work offers, warnings, a few things that don't fit either category.

fixtures.look: A wide corkboard covered in layered notices: shift assignments, food rationing updates, a missing persons notice with a photo, two work offers with no takers, and a handwritten request: 'Anyone with Earth seeds — even partial packs — please see Mira in the settler quarter.'
fixtures.examine: The deepest layers of the board go back months. The oldest notices are from when the colony still had optimistic handwriting. Newer additions are shorter, more direct. The missing persons notices accumulate without being removed. There are eleven of them.

areas.name: The Canteen — Back Porch
areas.description: A rough outdoor addition — salvaged panels for walls, no roof, a few mismatched chairs facing east toward the alien scrubland beyond the perimeter. People come here to think, to drink something they brought from Earth and won't say what it is, or to watch the sky without walls cutting off the view of The Eye. It is quieter than anywhere inside. The Eye hangs where it always does. The warmth and noise of the mess hall carry through the door to the east.

areas.name: Engineering Bay — Main Workshop
areas.description: The largest interior space in the settlement and the loudest. Fabrication benches, repair stations, hanging tools, and the smell of burnt metal mixed with something biological nobody has identified but everyone has agreed to ignore. This is where things get fixed, built, and occasionally invented. Chief Engineer Dax runs a strict shop. Intense heat and the ring of hammered metal come from the north. Component bins and circuit boards cover a bench to the south. A wide yard of salvaged hardware opens to the west.

areas.name: Engineering Bay — The Forge
areas.description: A proper forge — the colony's only metalworking station capable of serious fabrication. It runs on salvaged fuel cells and something Dax built herself that Corporate has asked about twice and received evasive answers. The heat is considerable. The mineral deposits from the ejecta field have kept it supplied, barely. When the forge is running, you can hear it from the street.

state_lines.default: The forge sits cold and banked, tools hung in their places on the wall behind it.
state_lines.cold: The forge sits cold and banked, tools hung in their places on the wall behind it.
state_lines.warm: The forge radiates stored heat — recently used, not yet cold.
state_lines.hot: The forge is running, its fuel cells cycling audibly, the heat significant from three meters away.

fixtures.look: A stone and steel forge built to Dax's design, modified three times since first construction. The fuel cell housing is the modification Corporate asked about. The ask was politely declined. Tongs, hammers, and shaping tools hang from a rack above.
fixtures.examine: The forge processes the high-iron ejecta minerals from the debris field into usable alloys. The process is inefficient by Earth standards and irreplaceable by any standard available on this planet. Dax keeps the specification in her head and nowhere else.

areas.name: Engineering Bay — Electronics Bench
areas.description: Component-level electronics work — comm units, sensors, corporate equipment that wasn't designed to be repaired in field conditions. The bench is covered in organized chaos: labeled bins, schematics pinned to the walls, the remains of three separate projects in various states of completion. The person who works here is rarely at the bench and always seems to know exactly what state everything is in.

areas.name: Engineering Bay — Salvage Yard
areas.description: An outdoor lot where the remains of failed equipment, crashed vehicles, and anything stripped for parts accumulates. It looks like chaos. It is carefully organized chaos — Dax knows exactly where every component is and will tell you this at length. The mineral-rich debris from the ejecta field has given the salvage operation more raw material than it can process. The workshop entrance is east.

areas.name: Medical Facility — Reception
areas.description: The best-equipped building in Firstfall, because Corporate needs its workforce functional. The waiting area has more chairs than patients most days — a reminder of how many people used to be here. The triage board on the wall tracks current cases. Clinical sounds and antiseptic carry from the north. Through a passage to the east, sensor arrays and stacked data printouts are visible through the window. A gate in the perimeter wall is to the south.

areas.name: Medical Facility — Treatment Bay
areas.description: Six beds, real diagnostic equipment, and the kind of quiet efficiency that comes from having handled more serious injuries than the facility was designed for. Chief Medic Osei runs a clean room. The list of things that have come through this door in a year would be useful information for Corporate's risk models, which is why it hasn't been sent to them in its complete form. A pharmacy is accessible to the east.

areas.name: Medical Facility — Pharmacy
areas.description: Locked cabinets, a dispensing terminal, and a handwritten cross-reference chart on the wall comparing Earth pharmaceutical nomenclature with what can be synthesized locally. Some things come off the shuttle. Some things come from the planet — sourced through channels that don't appear in the official supply chain. The pharmacist doesn't ask which kind you prefer.

areas.name: East Gate
areas.description: A quieter gate than the north entrance — used for coastal access and the occasional scouting run along the shoreline. The terrain beyond is alien scrubland where the ejecta debris field thins and the unaltered ecosystem begins. The Coherence pressure is noticeably higher here than inside the walls. Gate Watch Fen stands her post without comment. Alien vegetation presses against the fence from the east.

areas.name: Barracks — New Arrival Wing
areas.description: The bunk room for new arrivals — rows of cots, personal lockers, and the particular atmosphere of a space shared by people who don't know each other yet. Half the bunks are empty. They were not always empty. Your assigned bunk is here. A common area with voices and warm light is north. A heavy, restricted gate with a Corporate checkpoint is west.

areas.name: Barracks — Common Room
areas.description: A small common area with mismatched furniture, a card table, and a data terminal nobody uses officially and everyone uses to access the unsanctioned colony information network that technically does not exist. The conversations here are more honest than anything in the briefing rooms. People talk here the way people talk when they know they might not have many chances left to talk.

areas.name: West Gate
areas.description: A restricted gate — Corporate checkpoint, heavy mechanism, Corporal Westing who has declined every attempt to talk him into opening it without proper clearance. Beyond the gate, the continental interior: dense alien ecosystem, unbroken Coherence, no established paths, and nothing that has learned to tolerate humans yet. The wall top extends north along the perimeter.

areas.name: Settler Quarter — Main Street
areas.description: The organic section of the settlement — buildings assembled from salvage and local materials by people who needed space and built it themselves. Warmer than the Corporate prefabs, more complicated, more alive. The smell here is different: wood smoke, cooking, and something from the south wall that the Adapted tend and that nobody else fully understands. An informal gathering space is west, louder and more lived-in than any Corporate building. An open-air trading area with no posted prices runs east. A small cultivated garden presses against the south wall.

areas.name: Settler Quarter — Meeting House
areas.description: Settler governance — informal, loud, and more functional than it looks. The meeting house is a salvaged prefab module expanded twice with timber framing and once with something that might be coral from the bay. Decisions are made here by whoever shows up and argues long enough. Elder Rourke is usually in the corner chair.

areas.name: Settler Quarter — Unofficial Market
areas.description: No licenses, no corporate oversight, no fixed prices. Settlers trade what they find, make, grow, or carry with them from Earth. The selection is unpredictable — practical tools, alien materials, things that came off the shuttle and were never declared to the depot, and occasionally something nobody can explain the origin of. A more deliberate space — carved doorframe, quieter atmosphere — stands further to the east.

areas.name: Settler Quarter — Wall Garden
areas.description: A small cultivated space at the base of the south wall, tended by two of the Adapted and occasionally by someone Synchronized who visits without announcement. The plants here are local — not Earth varieties, not the aggressive ecosystem beyond the walls, but something in between: alien flora coaxed into something almost domestic. The Coherence is stronger here than anywhere else inside the settlement. On quiet afternoons you can almost feel it attending.

state_lines.default: The wall plants trail along the prefab seams, still in the breathless air.
state_lines.calm: The wall plants trail along the prefab seams, still in the breathless air.
state_lines.elevated: The wall plants lean east in unison, a slow arc with no wind to explain it.
state_lines.high: The wall plants have turned toward the gate, every stem oriented the same direction, attending.

fixtures.look: Alien flora growing up the interior face of the south wall — encouraged, not wild. The stems are jointed in a way that vertebrates use. The leaves are palmate, the wrong number of lobes. They have been here since the third month of the colony.
fixtures.examine: The Adapted who tend this garden have never explained what they are doing here, only that it matters. The plants respond to contact differently than plant behavior accounts for. They are not aggressive. They are not passive. The word that fits is 'attentive.'

areas.name: The Orthodox Gathering — The Threshold
areas.description: The entrance to the Orthodox space — not a building Corporate built and not one that looks like it was built in a hurry. The doorframe is carved with patterns that researchers have been trying to classify for months. Keeper Ysolde stands here most hours of the day, assessing everyone who approaches with a patience that is either deeply spiritual or deeply unnerving. The open trading area is to the west. Those the Keeper judges ready pass north.

areas.name: The Orthodox Gathering — The Hall
areas.description: The Orthodox meeting space — simple, deliberate, and quiet in a way that has nothing to do with sound dampening. The air here is different. The Coherence signal, suppressed everywhere else in the settlement by human infrastructure, is present in this room in a way that registered on three separate instruments before the researchers were asked to stop measuring. Speaker Davan is often here.

state_lines.default: A simple arrangement on a low table — stone, bone, and something from the bay — catches the light in a way that doesn't quite account for the angle of the source.
state_lines.calm: A simple arrangement on a low table — stone, bone, and something from the bay — catches the light in a way that doesn't quite account for the angle of the source.
state_lines.elevated: The arrangement on the low table casts shadows that don't track the light source.
state_lines.high: The arrangement on the low table is perfectly still. The room feels occupied by something that is not in it.

fixtures.look: Three objects on a circular table of local timber: a stone from the ejecta field, smoothed but not shaped; a curved fragment of something organic that is not bone but has the weight of one; and a sealed container of bay water, its surface catching light from an angle that changes when you're not looking at it directly.
fixtures.examine: The Orthodox do not explain the arrangement, but they do not discourage examination. The stone resonates differently than its composition accounts for. The organic fragment cannot be identified by any instrument the Seekers have applied to it. The water has not evaporated in eleven months.

areas.name: Research Station — Main Lab
areas.description: A converted prefab module covered inside and out with sensor equipment, sample containers, printed data sheets, and three whiteboards so dense with notation they look like a different language. Corporate tolerates the Seekers because their research occasionally produces useful findings. The Seekers tolerate Corporate because Corporate has the quantum comms relay. A ladder leads up to the roof.

areas.name: Research Station — The Roof
areas.description: A sensor array platform elevated above the surrounding buildings, the highest vantage point in the settlement. The view is unobstructed: the full sweep of the harbor to the south, the debris field running north, alien scrubland east, and beyond the west wall the dark mass of the continental interior pressing against the perimeter. The Eye is always visible from here. Dr. Okafor-Sinh comes here to think.

areas.name: Wall Walk — Northwest Section
areas.description: The walkable top of the perimeter wall between the north and west gates. The wall is wide enough for two people and has a railing that inspires more confidence than it deserves. From here the contrast is stark — the battered prefab settlement inside, and outside the alien terrain pressing in from every direction. Resource runners can be spotted heading north into the debris field on most mornings. A working gate with an active checkpoint is east. A restricted gate with a Corporate security post is south.
